qa_pairs.question: What is Abaqus FEA?
qa_pairs.answer: Abaqus FEA is a software suite for finite element analysis and computer-aided engineering.

qa_pairs.question: Are Abaqus software modules available on the clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, Abaqus software modules are available on our clusters, but you must provide your own license.

qa_pairs.question: How do I configure my Abaqus license on a cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: To configure your account on a cluster, log in and create a file named `$HOME/.licenses/abaqus.lic`.

qa_pairs.question: What should the `abaqus.lic` file contain for configuring my license?
qa_pairs.answer: The `abaqus.lic` file should contain the line `prepend_path("ABAQUSLM_LICENSE_FILE","port@server")`, with `port@server` replaced by your flexlm port number and server IP address or fully qualified hostname.

qa_pairs.question: How should I configure the license file if I want to use legacy Abaqus version 6.14.1?
qa_pairs.answer: If you want to use legacy version 6.14.1, replace `ABAQUSLM_LICENSE_FILE` with `LM_LICENSE_FILE` in your `abaqus.lic` file.

qa_pairs.question: What additional steps are required if my Abaqus license hasn't been set up for use on an Alliance cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: Additional configuration changes by the Alliance system administrator and your local system administrator will need to be done to ensure flexlm and vendor TCP ports are reachable from compute nodes.

qa_pairs.question: Whom should I contact for help setting up my Abaqus license for use on an Alliance cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: You should write to technical support for assistance.

qa_pairs.question: What information should I include when contacting technical support for Abaqus license setup on an Alliance cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: You should include the flexlm port number, static vendor port number, and the IP address of your Abaqus license server.

qa_pairs.question: What happens after I provide the necessary license information to technical support for an Alliance cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: You will be sent a list of cluster IP addresses so that your administrator can open the local server firewall to allow connections from the cluster on both ports.

qa_pairs.question: Is a special license agreement required to use a local Abaqus license remotely on Alliance hardware?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, a special license agreement must generally be negotiated and signed by SIMULIA and your institution.

qa_pairs.question: How is the `abaqus/2026` module configured by default regarding license servers?
qa_pairs.answer: The `abaqus/2026` module is configured by default to work with Simulia `FLEXnet` license servers, such as the free SHARCNET license server.

qa_pairs.question: How can I use a local DSLS-based institutional license server for Abaqus?
qa_pairs.answer: To use a local `DSLS` based institutional license server, you should create two small text files, `abaqus_v6.env` and `DSLicSrv.txt`, in your simulation submit directory.

qa_pairs.question: What should be the content of the `abaqus_v6.env` file for a DSLS license server?
qa_pairs.answer: The `abaqus_v6.env` file should contain: `license_server_type=DSLS` and `dsls_license_config="DSLicSrv.txt"`.

qa_pairs.question: What should be the content of the `DSLicSrv.txt` file for a DSLS license server?
qa_pairs.answer: The `DSLicSrv.txt` file should contain: `YOUR-SERVER-HOSTNAME:PORT-NUMBER`.

qa_pairs.question: When are the `abaqus_v6.env` and `DSLicSrv.txt` files read?
qa_pairs.answer: These files will be read automatically when Abaqus starts running to reconfigure itself accordingly.

qa_pairs.question: Which new Abaqus module has been installed into the default `StdEnv/2023` environment?
qa_pairs.answer: A new module for `abaqus/2026` is now installed into the default `StdEnv/2023` environment.

qa_pairs.question: What issue does the new `abaqus/2026` module resolve?
qa_pairs.answer: This new version resolves the `*** buffer overflow detected ***` error with `abaqus/2021` on all recent clusters.

qa_pairs.question: Have the Slurm scripts on the wiki page been updated for `abaqus/2026`?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, each Slurm script on this wiki page has been updated to work with both `abaqus/2026` and `abaqus/2021` where possible.

qa_pairs.question: What should researchers do with their personal Slurm scripts after the `abaqus/2026` module installation?
qa_pairs.answer: All personal Slurm scripts should likewise be updated by researchers.

qa_pairs.question: What does the initial `abaqus/2026` module contain?
qa_pairs.answer: The `abaqus/2026` module contains the initial `Abaqus 2026 Golden` release.

qa_pairs.question: What is the next `abaqus/2026` module that will be installed?
qa_pairs.answer: Another module named `abaqus/2026.2606` containing `Abaqus 2026 FP.CFA.2606` level updates will be installed next.

qa_pairs.question: What types of prototype Slurm scripts are available for Abaqus job submission?
qa_pairs.answer: Prototype Slurm scripts are available for submitting thread and MPI-based parallel simulations to single or multiple compute nodes.

qa_pairs.question: Which Slurm scripts are generally sufficient for most users?
qa_pairs.answer: Most users will find it sufficient to use one of the project directory scripts provided in the single node computing sections.

qa_pairs.question: What is the purpose of the optional `memory=` argument in the Slurm scripts?
qa_pairs.answer: The optional `memory=` argument is intended for larger memory or problematic jobs where the 3072MB offset value may require tuning.

qa_pairs.question: How can one obtain a listing of all Abaqus command line arguments?
qa_pairs.answer: A listing of all Abaqus command line arguments can be obtained by loading an Abaqus module and running: `abaqus -help | less`.

qa_pairs.question: Which script should be used for single node jobs running less than one day?
qa_pairs.answer: Single node jobs that run less than one day should use the project directory script located in the first tab.

qa_pairs.question: Which scripts should be used for single node jobs running for more than a day?
qa_pairs.answer: Single node jobs that run for more than a day should use one of the restart scripts.

qa_pairs.question: How can jobs that create large restart files benefit from local disk writing?
qa_pairs.answer: Jobs that create large restart files will benefit by writing to the local disk through the use of the `SLURM_TMPDIR` environment variable, utilized in the temporary directory scripts.

qa_pairs.question: What is the primary purpose of the provided restart scripts?
qa_pairs.answer: The restart scripts shown here will continue jobs that have been terminated early for some reason.

qa_pairs.question: What are common reasons for job failures that restart scripts can address?
qa_pairs.answer: Job failures can occur if a job reaches its maximum requested runtime before completing and is killed by the queue, or if the compute node the job was running on crashed due to an unexpected hardware failure.

qa_pairs.question: How can other types of job restarts be achieved?
qa_pairs.answer: Other restart types are possible by further tailoring of the input file to continue a job with additional steps or change the analysis.
